_meta.description: Case lifecycle, resolution, merging, routing rules, and case configuration.

mcq.q: Which Dynamics 365 Customer Service entity is the primary record for tracking a customer's issue or request?
mcq.explanation: Cases (also called Incidents) are the central entity in Dynamics 365 Customer Service. All customer issues, service requests, and support interactions are tracked as cases.
wrongExplanations: Activities (calls, emails, tasks) are child records linked to a case — they are not the primary tracking record.
Queue Items are wrappers that place records in queues for routing — they reference a case but are not the case itself.
Entitlements define support terms (hours, cases) for a customer — they govern case limits but are not the issue record.

mcq.q: A customer calls in and an agent creates a case directly from the phone call activity. Which feature enables this workflow?
mcq.explanation: The Convert to Case action on Activity records allows agents to create a case directly from a phone call, email, or other activity, linking the activity to the new case automatically.
wrongExplanations: Entitlement Channels define which contact channels (phone, email, web) decrement against an entitlement — not case creation from activities.
Case Creation Rules automatically create cases from inbound emails — agents use Convert to Case for manual activity conversion.
Unified Routing assigns cases to queues and agents based on rules — it is not used to convert activities to cases.

msRef.label: Create a case from an activity

mcq.q: When an agent resolves a case in Dynamics 365 Customer Service, which activity record is automatically created to capture the resolution details?
mcq.explanation: Resolving a case creates a Case Resolution Activity record that stores the resolution type, billable and total time, and the resolution description. This activity is linked to the case for audit and reporting.
wrongExplanations: Tasks are manual activities created by agents — they are not created automatically on case resolution.
Appointments are calendar-based activities — not created on case resolution.
Notes are manual text entries — resolution details are captured in the Resolution Activity, not a note.

mcq.q: Which case status reason is set when a case is resolved in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
mcq.explanation: When a case is resolved, its Status Reason is set to Problem Solved (the default resolution reason). Administrators can add custom resolution reasons, but Problem Solved is the system default.
wrongExplanations: Closed is a status, not a status reason. The Status becomes Resolved when a case is resolved.
Resolved is the case Status (not Status Reason) that is set when the case is resolved.
Completed is used in other entities like tasks — not the default case resolution reason.

mcq.q: A company wants agents to confirm that they have communicated the resolution to the customer before closing a case. Which configuration achieves this?
options: Add a mandatory field to the Case Resolution dialog
Create a Business Rule that enforces the field before allowing status change
Use a Business Process Flow stage that requires a Customer Notified step before moving to Resolved
Configure an SLA action to block resolution until the customer responds
mcq.explanation: Business Process Flows (BPFs) guide agents through required stages. By adding a Customer Notified step with a required field in the stage preceding resolution, agents cannot advance to the Resolved stage without completing that step.
wrongExplanations: The Case Resolution dialog has limited customisation — adding mandatory fields there is not straightforward.
A Business Rule can enforce field requirements on a form but does not block the resolution dialog action itself.
SLA actions fire on time-based triggers — they cannot block resolution based on a customer response condition.

mcq.q: Which setting controls whether a resolved case can be reactivated by agents in Dynamics 365 Customer Service?
options: Case Settings in Customer Service Hub admin
Service Configuration Settings — specifically the Allow resolved cases to be reactivated option
Security role privileges on the Case entity
Business Rule on the Case form
mcq.explanation: The Service Configuration Settings in the Customer Service admin centre include an option to allow or disallow reactivation of resolved cases. When disabled, agents cannot reopen resolved cases and must create a new case instead.
wrongExplanations: Customer Service Hub admin is where this is found, but the specific setting is within Service Configuration Settings.
Security roles control who can perform actions but do not toggle the system-level reactivation capability.
Business Rules enforce form logic — they cannot block the system case reactivation action at the platform level.

mcq.q: An agent cancels a case rather than resolving it. Which status reason is applied by default?
mcq.explanation: When an agent uses the Cancel Case action, the Status becomes Cancelled and the default Status Reason is Duplicate. Administrators can add additional cancellation reasons to accommodate different cancellation scenarios.
wrongExplanations: Cancelled is the Status, not the Status Reason, when a case is cancelled.
Merged is a separate status reason used when two cases are merged together — not the default cancellation reason.
Withdrawn is not a default status reason in Dynamics 365 Customer Service.

mcq.q: Which feature allows a supervisor to combine two related cases into a single primary case and automatically close the secondary one?
mcq.explanation: Case Merging combines two case records into one. The secondary case is cancelled with a Merged status reason, and all activities, notes, and child records from both cases are associated with the primary case.
wrongExplanations: Case Routing assigns cases to queues or agents — it does not combine cases.
Case Linking associates related cases together for reference but keeps both cases active — it does not close either one.
Case Escalation is an informal term for increasing priority — it is not a specific merge feature.

mcq.q: When configuring the Case Resolution dialog box, which admin setting allows you to require agents to provide a resolution description before closing a case?
options: Make resolution description required in the Case entity field settings
Enable Require description in resolution dialog in Service Configuration Settings
Add a business rule on the Case entity for the resolution field
Customise the Case Resolution Activity form to mark the field as required
mcq.explanation: The Service Configuration Settings in Customer Service admin include a toggle to require a resolution description in the resolution dialog. When enabled, agents cannot resolve a case without entering a description.
wrongExplanations: The Case entity field settings do not control the resolution dialog required field behaviour.
Business Rules operate on form logic — they do not intercept the resolution dialog popup.
Customising the Case Resolution Activity form affects the activity record after creation — not the resolution dialog prompt shown to agents.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each Dynamics 365 Customer Service component to its primary purpose.
zones: Defines time targets for case response and resolution
Defines support terms a customer is entitled to (cases or hours)
Automatically assigns cases to queues based on conditions
Provides hierarchical classification for cases and articles
Stores reusable solutions agents can link to cases

correctMapping.SLA: Defines time targets for case response and resolution
correctMapping.Entitlement: Defines support terms a customer is entitled to (cases or hours)
correctMapping.Routing Rule Set: Automatically assigns cases to queues based on conditions
correctMapping.Subject Tree: Provides hierarchical classification for cases and articles
correctMapping.Knowledge Article: Stores reusable solutions agents can link to cases

dragdrop.explanation: Each component plays a distinct role: SLAs enforce time targets; Entitlements define support contracts; Routing Rules manage queue assignment; Subject Trees classify content; Knowledge Articles capture solutions.

dragdrop.instruction: Match each case status and status reason to the correct lifecycle stage.
zones: Case is open and being worked on
Case has been completed or answered

correctMapping.Active / In Progress: Case is open and being worked on
correctMapping.Active / On Hold: Case is open and being worked on
correctMapping.Resolved / Problem Solved: Case has been completed or answered
correctMapping.Resolved / Information Provided: Case has been completed or answered

dragdrop.explanation: Active cases (In Progress, On Hold) are still being worked. Resolved cases (Problem Solved, Information Provided) have been answered or fixed. Cancelled/Duplicate cases were closed without a true resolution.

dragdrop.instruction: Classify each configuration action to the correct admin area where it is performed.
items: Add a subject to the Subject Tree
Set up a Record Creation Rule
zones: Power Platform admin centre or Security settings

correctMapping.Create a new security role: Power Platform admin centre or Security settings

dragdrop.explanation: Customer Service settings live in the CS admin centre. Security roles are managed in the Power Platform admin centre. Timeline card forms are customised through the Power Apps form editor.

casestudy.title: Contoso Support Centre Setup
casestudy.scenario: Contoso has recently licensed Dynamics 365 Customer Service and is setting up the system from scratch. The support centre has the following requirements: 1. All inbound emails to support@contoso.com must automatically create cases 2. Cases must be categorised using a standard product taxonomy 3. Agents should only be able to edit cases they personally own 4. Supervisors must be able to view and reassign all cases across the team 5. Cases must be resolved within 4 hours of creation, with a warning at 3 hours 6. Agents must confirm the customer was notified before resolving a case The admin team is configuring all of these requirements before going live.

questions.q: Agents must confirm the customer was notified before resolving a case. What is the best configuration approach?
options: Add a mandatory note to the Timeline before resolution is allowed
Use a Business Process Flow with a required Customer Notified field in the stage before Resolved
Configure the Case Resolution dialog to require a confirmation checkbox
Create a Power Automate flow that blocks resolution without a phone call activity
questions.explanation: A Business Process Flow with a required Customer Notified field in the final active stage ensures agents cannot advance to Resolved without confirming customer notification.
wrongExplanations: The Timeline does not have a built-in mechanism to block case resolution based on note presence.
The Case Resolution dialog can require a description but does not support custom confirmation checkboxes.
A Power Automate flow could block resolution via a plugin pattern but this is significantly more complex — BPF is the recommended out-of-box approach.
